attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

This approach provides a clean way to isolate and resolve the audio issues without blocking other development work, and establishes a pattern for handling similar asset issues in template projects.</narrative>

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>BGM Control Methods in GameView</title>
  <subtitle>GameView implements six methods for managing main game and free game background music lifecycle</subtitle>
  <facts>

    <fact>StopMainGameBgm() and StopFreeGameBgm() methods stop and reset BGM IDs</fact>
    <fact>PauseMainGameBgm() and ResumeMainGameBgm() methods control main game BGM playback state</fact>
    <fact>Both BGM types use instance IDs (m_bgmId and m_freeBgmId) tracked at -1 when not playing</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The GameView class implements a comprehensive BGM control system with six dedicated methods handling the complete lifecycle of background music. For the main game, PlayMainGameBgm(), StopMainGameBgm(), PauseMainGameBgm(), and ResumeMainGameBgm() manage the MG_Bgm audio clip through a tracked m_bgmId instance. Similarly, PlayFreeGameBgm() and StopFreeGameBgm() handle the FG_Bgm clip via m_freeBgmId. Each Play method checks if the BGM is already playing (ID !== -1) before starting playback, and Stop methods properly reset the ID to -1. This architecture ensures only one instance of each BGM type plays at a time. When commenting out BGM references, these method implementations will need attention, particularly the AudioManager.Play lines that reference AudioManager.AudioClips.MG_Bgm and FG_Bgm.</narrative>

音效來源，可以是字串鍵名、AudioClip 物件或音效資料物件\\n     * @param loop 是否循環播放，背景音樂通常設為 true\\n     * @param cb 播放完成後的回調函式\\n     * @param volume 播放音量，範圍 0-1，預設使用系統音量\\n     * @returns 返回音效的唯一 ID，如果因並發限制而無法播放則返回 -1\\n   * AudioManager.PlayBackGround({ FileName: \\\"bgm\\\", Clip: audioClip }, true);\\n     * ```\\n     */\\n    static PlayBackGround(input: string, loop?: boolean, cb?: () => void, volume?: number): number;\\n    static PlayBackGround(input: AudioClip, loop?: boolean, cb?: () => void, volume?: number): number;\\n    static PlayBackGround(input: { FileName: string, Clip: AudioClip }, loop?: boolean, cb?: () => void, volume?: number): number;\\n    static PlayBackGround( input: string | AudioClip | { FileName: string, Clip: AudioClip }, loop?: boolean, cb?: () => void, volume?: number ): number {\\n        let audio: AudioClip;\\n        let audioKey: string;\\n\\n        if (typeof input === 'string') {\\n            audio = this.GetAudioClip(input);\\n            audioKey = input + '_bg';\\n        } else if (typeof input === 'object' && 'Clip' in input) {\\n            audio = input.Clip;\\n            audioKey = input.FileName + '_bg';\\n        } else {\\n            audio = input;\\n            audioKey = (audio?.name || 'unknown') + '_bg';\\n        }\\n\\n        // 檢查並發限制\\n        const canPlay = this.CheckConcurrentLimit(audioKey);\\n        if (!canPlay) {\\n            console.warn(`背景音效 \\\"${audioKey}\\\" 因並發限制而無法播放`);\\n            return -1;\\n        }\\n\\n        // 包裝回調函數以實現自動清理\\n        const wrappedCallback = () => {\\n            if (cb) cb();\\n wrappedCallback, volume);\\n\\n        if (audioId !== -1) {\\n            // 記錄播放實例\\n            this.AddPlayingInstance(audioKey, audioId);\\n        }\\n\\n        return audioId;\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * 停止音效播放\\n     * @description 根據音效 ID 停止指定的音效播放，並自動清理追蹤記錄\\n     * @param id 音效的唯一 ID，由 Play 或 PlayBackGround 方法返回\\n     * @param audioName 音效名稱，用於額外確認，避免停錯音效（可選）\\n     * @example\\n 動態調整指定音效的播放音量\\n     * @param id 音效的唯一 ID\\n     * @param vol 音量值，範圍 0-1，0 為靜音，1 為最大音量\\n     * @example\\n     * ```typescript\\n     * const id = AudioManager.Play(\\\"G97_Main_Bgm\\\", true);\\n     * AudioManager.SetVolume(id, 0.5); // 設定為 50% 音量\\n     * ```\\n     */\\n    static SetVolume( id: number, vol: number ): void {\\n        soundAudioManager.SetVolume(id, vol);\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * 取得音效音量\\n     * @description 獲取指定音效的當前音量\\n     * @param id 音效的唯一 ID\\n     * @returns 返回音量值，範圍 0-1\\n     * @example\\n     * ```typescript\\n     * const id = === 1) {\\n     *     console.log(\\\"音效播放中\\\");\\n     * }\\n     * ```\\n     */\\n    static GetAudioStateById( id: number ): number {\\n        return soundAudioManager.GetAudioStateById(id);\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * 設定音效淡入效果\\n     * @description 讓指定音效以淡入方式播放，音量漸漸增加\\n     * @param id 音效的唯一 ID\\n     * @param fadeInTime 淡入時間（秒），預設值由系統決定\\n     * @param maxVolume 淡入後的最大音量，範圍 0-1，預設值由系統決定\\n     * @example\\n     * ```typescript\\n     * const id = AudioManager.Play(\\\"G97_Main_Bgm\\\", true);\\n     * AudioManager.SetMusicFadeIn(id, 3.0, 0.8); // 3秒淡入到80%音量\\n     * ```\\n     */\\n    static SetMusicFadeIn( id: number, fadeInTime?: number, maxVolume?: number ): void {\\n        soundAudioManager.SetMusicFadeIn(id, fadeInTime, maxVolume);\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n AudioManager.Play(\\\"G97_Main_Bgm\\\", true);\\n     * AudioManager.SetMusicFadeOut(id, 2.0); // 2秒淡出\\n     * ```\\n     */\\n    static SetMusicFadeOut( id: number, fadeOutTime?: number ): void {\\n        soundAudioManager.SetMusicFadeOut(id, fadeOutTime);\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * 設定音效配置檔案\\n     * @description 載入音效混音器配置檔案，用於設定音效的混音參數\\n     * @param audioMixerFile 音效混音器配置檔案 (JsonAsset)\\n     * @warning 如果已設定過其他配置檔案，建議先呼叫 ClearSetting() 清除舊設定\\n     * @example\\n     * ```typescript\\n     * // 載入配置檔案\\n     * AudioManager.SetSettingJson(audioMixerAsset);\\n     * ```\\n     */\\n    static SetSettingJson( audioMixerFile: JsonAsset ): void {\\n        soundAudioManager.SetSettingJson(audioMixerFile);\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * 清除音效配置檔案\\n     * @description 清除當前的音效配置檔案，釋放相關資源\\n     * @usage 在換場景或設定新的音效配置檔案前使用\\n     * @example\\n     * ```typescript\\n     * // 場景切換時清除配置\\n     * AudioManager.ClearSetting();\\n     * ```\\n     */\\n    static ClearSetting(): void {\\n        soundAudioManager.ClearSetting();\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * 新增音效片段清單\\n     * @description 將音效片段陣列新增到音效管理器中\\n     * @param audioList 音效片段陣列\\n     * @note ```\\n     */\\n    static AddAudioClips( audioList: AudioClip[] ): void {\\n        soundAudioManager.AddAudioClips(audioList);\\n    }\\n\\n    // =================================================================\\n    // 並發限制管理方法 (Concurrent Limit Management Methods)\\n    // =================================================================\\n\\n    /**\\n     * 設定音效並發限制\\n     * @description 為指定音效設定同時間可播放的最大數量\\n     * @param audioKey 音效鍵名，對應 AudioClips 中的鍵名\\n     * @param maxCount 最大並發數量，設為 -1 表示無限制\\n     * @example\\n     * ```typescript\\n     * // 設定技能爆炸音效同時間最多只能播放 3 個\\n     * AudioManager.SetConcurrentLimit(\\\"skill_explosion\\\", 3);\\n     *\\n     * // 設定按鈕音效同時間最多只能播放 1 個\\n     * AudioManager.SetConcurrentLimit(\\\"button_click\\\", 1);\\n     *\\n     * // 移除限制\\n     * AudioManager.SetConcurrentLimit(\\\"background_music\\\", -1);\\n     * ```\\n     */\\n    static SetConcurrentLimit(audioKey: string, maxCount: number): void {\\n        if (maxCount <= 0 && maxCount !== -1) {\\n            console.warn(`無效的並發限制數量: ${maxCount}，應該大於 0 或設為 -1`);\\n            return;\\n        }\\n\\n        if (maxCount === -1) {\\n            this.m_concurrentLimits.delete(audioKey);\\n        } else {\\n            this.m_concurrentLimits.set(audioKey, maxCount);\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * 設定預設並發限制\\n     * @description 設定所有音效的預設並發限制，對於沒有特別設定的音效生效\\n     * @param maxCount 預設最大並發數量，設為 -1 表示無限制\\n     * @example\\n     * ```typescript\\n     * // 設定所有音效預設最多同時播放 5 個\\n     * AudioManager.SetDefaultConcurrentLimit(5);\\n     *\\n     * // 移除預設限制\\n     * ```xml
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>AudioClips Dictionary Structure in AudioManager</title>
  <subtitle>MG_Bgm and FG_Bgm defined in AudioClips dictionary with FileName and Clip properties</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>AudioManager.ts lines 36-37 define MG_Bgm and FG_Bgm in static AudioClips dictionary</fact>
    <fact>MG_Bgm entry specifies FileName as "main_bgm" with Clip initialized to null</fact>
    <fact>FG_Bgm entry specifies FileName as "fg_bgm" with Clip initialized to null</fact>
    <fact>AudioClips dictionary uses key-value pairs where key matches audio asset name</fact>
    <fact>Clip property starts null and requires loading before playback</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The AudioManager implements a centralized AudioClips dictionary that stores all audio asset definitions. Each entry contains a FileName property (the actual audio file name without extension) and a Clip property (the loaded AudioClip object, initially null). The MG_Bgm (main game background music) and FG_Bgm (free game background music) entries follow this pattern at lines 36-37. This dictionary structure allows other code to reference audio clips via AudioManager.AudioClips.MG_Bgm, providing a typed, centralized registry. When commenting out BGM references, these two lines in the AudioClips definition are the source declarations that must be addressed, along with all downstream usage in GameView and documentation.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>G:/Cocos_Project/uk_slot_template/assets/Script/Audio/AudioManager.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```

content.thinking:
The user edited AudioManager.ts to comment out the MG_Bgm and FG_Bgm entries in the AudioClips dictionary. They added a comprehensive comment explaining:
1. The template doesn't include bgm audio files (each game has different audio)
2. When starting a new project, after adding main_bgm/fg_bgm audio files, uncomment these lines
3. Also need to uncomment the Play calls in GameView's PlayMainGameBgm/PlayFreeGameBgm methods

This is the first step in the BGM commenting strategy decided earlier.

  <title>BGM Audio Clips Commented Out in AudioManager</title>
  <subtitle>MG_Bgm and FG_Bgm entries commented with instructions for re-enabling in new projects</subtitle>
  <facts>

    <fact>Added comment explaining template excludes bgm files because each game has different audio</fact>
    <fact>Instructions added to uncomment AudioClips entries and GameView Play calls when adding audio files</fact>
    <fact>Comment directs developers to also modify PlayMainGameBgm and PlayFreeGameBgm methods in GameView</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The AudioManager AudioClips dictionary has been modified to comment out the MG_Bgm and FG_Bgm entries, removing the default background music definitions from the template. A detailed Chinese comment was added explaining the rationale: the template intentionally excludes bgm audio files since each game project will have unique audio assets. The comment provides clear re-enablement instructions for developers creating new game projects: after adding main_bgm/fg_bgm audio files to their project, they should uncomment these AudioClips entries as well as the corresponding Play() method calls inside GameView's PlayMainGameBgm and PlayFreeGameBgm methods. This change transforms the template into a more flexible starting point that won't fail due to missing audio assets, while preserving the complete BGM infrastructure code for easy activation.</narrative>

  </files_modified>
</observation>

   /**\\n     * @ch 預處理盤面資料：統一符號數量並保留完整 CSymbol 結構\\n     * @param serverData 原始服務器資料\\n     * @returns 預處理後的完整盤面資料（包含真實符號和假符號標記）\\n     */\\n    private PreprocessPlateDataForServer( serverData: {\\n            return; // 尚未初始化\\n        }\\n        \\n        for ( let col = 0; col < Game_Define.COL; col++ )\\n        {\\n            const actualSymbolCount = this.GetTargetSymbolCount( col content.thinking:
The user is showing me two edit operations on GameView.ts file. Both edits are commenting out BGM (background music) playback calls in the PlayMainGameBgm() and PlayFreeGameBgm() methods.

First edit:
- In PlayMainGameBgm() method (around line 1367)
- Commented out: `this.m_bgmId = AudioManager.Play( AudioManager.AudioClips.MG_Bgm, true );`
- Added comment: "模板不含 bgm 音檔，起新專案補音檔後解除註解（見 AudioManager.AudioClips）"
- Translation: "Template doesn't contain bgm audio files, uncomment after adding audio files to new project (see AudioManager.AudioClips)"

Second edit:
- In PlayFreeGameBgm() method (around line 1412)
- Commented out: `this.m_freeBgmId = AudioManager.Play( AudioManager.AudioClips.FG_Bgm, true );`
- Added same comment about template not containing bgm files

This completes the BGM commenting strategy mentioned earlier. Now both the AudioClips dictionary definitions AND the actual Play() method calls are commented out, making the template truly BGM-free while preserving all the infrastructure for easy re-enablement.

   /**\\n     * @ch 預處理盤面資料：統一符號數量並保留完整 CSymbol 結構\\n     * @param serverData 原始服務器資料\\n     * @returns 預處理後的完整盤面資料（包含真實符號和假符號標記）\\n     */\\n    private PreprocessPlateDataForServer( serverData: {\\n            return; // 尚未初始化\\n        }\\n        \\n        for ( let col = 0; col < Game_Define.COL; col++ )\\n        {\\n            const actualSymbolCount = this.GetTargetSymbolCount( col return;\\n        }\\n\\n        this.SpinAll( dt, NORMAL_COLUMNS );\\n\\n        if ( this.StopTime === 0 || this.m_time <= this.StopTime )\\n        {\\n            return;\\n        }\\n\\n    this.m_reelSoundId = null;\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 旋轉上、中、下所有輪帶，第二個參數為控制單輪\\n     * @param dt 時間\\n     * @param cols 列索引 (0 ~ Game_Define.COL-1)\\n     */\\n    SpinAll( dt, content.thinking:
The user is modifying SlotReels.ts to derive arrays from Game_Define.COL instead of using hardcoded values. This is a series of changes to make the codebase more maintainable when the column count changes. Let me observe the changes:

1. NORMAL_COLUMNS changed from hardcoded `[0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5]` to `Array.from({ length: Game_Define.COL }, (_, i) => i)` - dynamic generation
2. m_reelYOffsetProperty changed from hardcoded `[0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0]` to `new Array(Game_Define.COL).fill(0)` - dynamic initialization
3. m_reelPositionOffset changed from hardcoded 6 Vec3 entries to `Array.from({ length: Game_Define.COL }, () => v3(0, 0))` - dynamic generation

This is part of the COL-derived backfill to the template project that was mentioned in the initial context. These changes make the code automatically adapt when Game_Define.COL changes, avoiding maintenance issues.

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>refactor</type>
  <title>SlotReels Arrays Derived from Game_Define.COL</title>
  <subtitle>Three hardcoded 6-element arrays now dynamically generated from Game_Define.COL constant</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>NORMAL_COLUMNS constant changed from hardcoded [0,1,2,3,4,5] to Array.from with Game_Define.COL length</fact>
    <fact>m_reelYOffsetProperty changed from 6-element array literal to new Array(Game_Define.COL).fill(0)</fact>
    <fact>m_reelPositionOffset changed from 6 v3(0,0) literals to Array.from generating Game_Define.COL elements</fact>
    <fact>All three changes eliminate hardcoded column count assumptions in SlotReels.ts</fact>
    <fact>Comment added to NORMAL_COLUMNS explaining avoidance of desync when plate size changes</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>SlotReels.ts has been refactored to remove hardcoded assumptions about a 6-column reel layout. Three arrays that previously contained six hardcoded elements have been converted to dynamically derive their size from Game_Define.COL. NORMAL_COLUMNS now uses Array.from to generate sequential column indices [0..COL-1], m_reelYOffsetProperty uses Array constructor with fill(0) for zero-initialized offsets, and m_reelPositionOffset uses Array.from with a factory function to generate COL instances of v3(0,0). This change is part of the "COL衍生灌回模專案" (COL-derived backfill to template project) strategy mentioned in the user's initial context. The refactor ensures that when Game_Define.COL is modified (e.g., changing from 6 to 5 columns), these arrays automatically adjust without requiring manual code changes, preventing synchronization bugs between the plate size configuration and reel infrastructure code.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>why-it-exists</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>G:/Cocos_Project/uk_slot_template/assets/Script/SlotReels.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```
